name: interp_of_entry_key_between_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_interp_of_entry_key_between_external_body(d: Directory, i: nat, va: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(va),
    ensures
        d.entry_base(i) <= va,
        va < d.next_entry_base(i),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_key_between(i, va);
}
reason: `lemma_interp_of_entry_key_between` is `external_body` — the bound that VA keys in an entry's interp fall within [entry_base, next_entry_base) is trusted without proof

name: interp_aux_contains_implies_entry_contains_external_body
reason: `lemma_interp_aux_contains_implies_interp_of_entry_contains` is `external_body` — the decomposition of interp_aux keys into individual entry contributions is trusted without proof

name: distinct_entries_disjoint_ranges
reason: Two applications of the external_body key-between axiom derive entry disjointness — if the bounds axiom were wrong, overlapping entries could silently shadow each other

name: remove_propagates_to_interp_aux
code:
proof fn phi_4_remove_propagates_to_interp_aux(d1: Directory, d2: Directory, idx: nat, vaddr: nat)
    requires
        d1.inv(),
        d2.inv(),
        idx < d1.entries.len(),
        d1.arch == d2.arch,
        d1.layer == d2.layer,
        d1.base_vaddr == d2.base_vaddr,
        d1.entries[idx as int].interp(d1.entry_base(idx)).contains_key(vaddr),
        d2.entries[idx as int].interp(d1.entry_base(idx)) == d1.entries[idx as int].interp(d1.entry_base(idx)).remove(vaddr),
        forall|j: nat| j < d1.entries.len() && j != idx
            ==> (#[trigger] d1.entries[j as int]).interp(d1.entry_base(j))
                        == d2.entries[j as int].interp(d1.entry_base(j)),
    ensures
        d2.interp_aux(0) === d1.interp_aux(0).remove(vaddr),
{
    d1.lemma_entries_interp_remove_implies_interp_aux_remove(d2, idx, vaddr, 0);
}
reason: Remove propagation depends on both external_body axioms — the verified proof uses key-between and contains-implies-entry-contains as unverified foundations

name: page_entry_key_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_page_entry_key_equals_base(d: Directory, i: nat, va: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(va),
    ensures
        va == d.entry_base(i),
{
}
reason: A page entry's singleton map has exactly one key (entry_base) — if va is in the map, it must equal the base; tests that the open spec unfolds correctly
